text:
HEMATOLOGY/BONE MARROW TRANSPLANT PROGRAM
OUTPATIENT FOLLOW-UP NOTE
12-MONTH POST-ALLOGENEIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT EVALUATION

ATTENDING: Hematology/BMT Attending
FELLOW: BMT Fellow
VISIT TYPE: Comprehensive Annual Transplant Evaluation

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 49-year-old Nigerian-American woman presenting for her formal one-year post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) evaluation. She is doing well overall, has returned to part-time nursing work, and comes in today for protocol-mandated surveillance, comprehensive laboratory assessment, pulmonary function testing, and bone marrow biopsy to confirm ongoing remission status.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Okonkwo was originally diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC) arising from a known antecedent myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS with refractory cytopenia and multilineage dysplasia, identified approximately 18 months prior to AML diagnosis). At diagnosis she presented with a WBC of 84,000/uL, hemoglobin 7.1 g/dL, platelets 28,000/uL, and 62% myeloblasts on bone marrow biopsy — a quite dramatic presentation. Molecular workup revealed a FLT3-ITD mutation with high allelic ratio (VAF 0.82), along with co-mutations in DNMT3A (R882H), ASXL1 (G646Wfs*12), SRSF2 (P95H), and RUNX1 (R204Q). Cytogenetics showed a normal female karyotype with a minor del(7q) clone remnant from the antecedent MDS. By ELN 2022 criteria this placed her firmly in the adverse-risk category, with allogeneic transplant in first remission as the clear treatment goal.

She received 7+3 induction chemotherapy with cytarabine and daunorubicin plus midostaurin (per RATIFY protocol) and achieved a morphologic and MRD-negative complete remission (CR) by day 30 bone marrow biopsy. Her induction course was complicated by febrile neutropenia with E. coli bacteremia (successfully treated with cefepime for 14 days), Grade 3 mucositis requiring morphine PCA and TPN support for approximately 8 days, and Grade 3 anemia/thrombocytopenia requiring transfusion support — all expected in the setting of myeloablative-intent 7+3. Midostaurin was well tolerated without dose reduction.

She went on to receive one cycle of high-dose cytarabine (HiDAC, 3 g/m2 q12h on days 1, 3, and 5) as consolidation, complicated by Grade 2 cerebellar ataxia and dysmetria after 5 of 6 planned doses (classic cytarabine neurotoxicity), with full neurologic recovery. Given prompt identification of a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor, she proceeded directly to transplant after one consolidation cycle. Pre-transplant restaging confirmed sustained MRD-negative remission both by PET-CT (no hypermetabolic disease) and bone marrow biopsy (MRD negative, FLT3-ITD and DNMT3A not detected by ddPCR).

Approximately four months after AML diagnosis, she underwent matched unrelated donor peripheral blood stem cell transplant (MUD alloHSCT) with a 10/10 HLA-matched CMV-seronegative male donor (she was CMV seropositive, D-/R+ mismatch). Conditioning: reduced-intensity with fludarabine/busulfan (Flu/Bu2, pharmacokinetic-targeted IV busulfan, AUC ~5,000 uM*min/day). GVHD prophylaxis: tacrolimus plus MTX (days +1, +3, +6, +11) plus rabbit ATG (Thymoglobulin) on days -3, -2, -1. Engraftment was prompt: ANC nadir recovery to >500/uL by day +16, platelets unsupported >20,000/uL by day +20. Day +30 chimerism showed 99% donor myeloid, 95% donor T-cell, with MRD-negative marrow.

Early post-transplant course was notable for two significant complications:
1. CMV reactivation at day +45 (peak PCR 1,840 IU/mL in a D-/R+ setting), treated pre-emptively with valganciclovir 900 mg BID with clearance by day +60, then stepped down to prophylaxis through day +100. No CMV end-organ disease developed.
2. Mild chronic GVHD emerging around day +90: lichenoid skin changes on the forearms and dorsal hands (~15% BSA), oral mucosa lichenoid changes with xerostomia, and borderline Schirmer's test (7 mm) reflecting mild dry eye syndrome. NIH Global Severity Score: mild. Managed with topical clobetasol for skin, dexamethasone oral rinse for mucosa, and cyclosporine eye drops plus artificial tears. Tacrolimus taper was held. No systemic steroids were required. At the day +100 evaluation, counts were fully recovering (WBC 4,800/uL, Hgb 11.9 g/dL, platelets 168,000/uL), chronic GVHD was stable and improving, and MRD remained negative.

At the 6-month evaluation (day +180), bone marrow biopsy confirmed ongoing MRD-negative complete remission, full donor chimerism (99% whole marrow, 98% T-cell, 99% myeloid), and molecular clearance of FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A R882H, and ASXL1 frameshift mutations by ddPCR. CT imaging showed resolved splenomegaly and no evidence of extramedullary disease.

Today she presents for the formal 12-month/one-year evaluation. She reports she has been doing really well. She returned to part-time nursing work about two months ago and is tolerating it without excessive fatigue. Her skin lichenoid changes have nearly resolved. She still notices some dryness of the mouth, particularly at night, but she is managing with saliva substitutes and pilocarpine (started at a prior visit). Her eye dryness has been adequately controlled with cyclosporine eye drops and artificial tears. She denies any new skin rashes, GI symptoms, dyspnea, or joint stiffness. She has had no fevers, infections, or hospitalizations since her last clinic visit 3 months ago. Her blood sugars have been well-controlled on metformin (recently rechecked HbA1c 6.8%), and her blood pressure has been acceptable on lisinopril. She feels her energy and quality of life have substantially improved compared to the first few months post-transplant.

No new complaints. Tacrolimus taper has been ongoing per protocol — she is currently on 1 mg daily, nearly at the point of discontinuation, which is excellent given her mild cGVHD picture.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: No fevers, chills, or night sweats. Energy levels substantially improved; mild baseline fatigue with prolonged activity. No significant unintentional weight change. Appetite normal.
Skin: Lichenoid changes on forearms/dorsal hands nearly resolved; occasional pruritus (mild, 1-2/10). No new rashes, ulcerations, or sclerodermatous changes.
Head/Eyes: Dry eyes, managed with drops; no pain or photophobia. No cataracts reported subjectively.
Mouth/ENT: Persistent xerostomia (mild), improved with pilocarpine and saliva substitutes. No active oral ulcerations or pain. No difficulty swallowing.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or edema.
Pulmonary: No dyspnea at rest. Mild exertional dyspnea with significant physical activity; improving with deconditioning recovery. No cough, wheezing, or hemoptysis.
Gastrointestinal: Regular bowel movements, no diarrhea or blood in stool. No nausea/vomiting. No abdominal cramping.
Genitourinary: Normal urinary function. Menstrual cycles: amenorrhea (expected post-transplant conditioning; reproductive endocrine not yet fully evaluated at this visit).
Musculoskeletal: No joint pain, stiffness, or contractures. No bone pain. No avascular necrosis symptoms (no hip, knee, or shoulder pain).
Neurological: No headaches, paresthesias, tremors, or cognitive changes. Neurologic symptoms from HiDAC (prior cerebellar toxicity) fully resolved.
Psychiatric: Mood good. Some residual anxiety around relapse, which is normal and improving with time and continued remission confirmation. Returns to normal social activities and work.
Endocrine: Type 2 DM well-controlled. No symptoms of hypothyroidism. No adrenal insufficiency symptoms (off steroids).

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY (Summary)
- AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), ELN adverse risk: FLT3-ITD (high allelic ratio), DNMT3A R882H, ASXL1, SRSF2, RUNX1 co-mutations; diagnosed at age 48. Status: sustained MRD-negative CR1 post-alloHSCT.
- Antecedent MDS (RCMD), observed without treatment for ~18 months prior to AML transformation.
- Matched unrelated donor peripheral blood alloHSCT (10/10 HLA-match, D-/R+ CMV), Flu/Bu RIC conditioning, tacrolimus/MTX/ATG GVHD prophylaxis.
- Mild chronic GVHD: skin (near-resolved), oral (improving xerostomia), dry eyes (stable on cyclosporine drops).
- CMV reactivation post-transplant, resolved on valganciclovir, no end-organ disease.
- Hypertension: managed with lisinopril.
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus: managed with metformin.
- Prior febrile neutropenia/E. coli bacteremia during induction (resolved).
- Grade 2 cytarabine cerebellar neurotoxicity during HiDAC consolidation (resolved).

MEDICATIONS
- Tacrolimus 1 mg PO daily (near taper completion, plan to discontinue per protocol)
- Metformin 1000 mg PO BID (type 2 DM)
- Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily (hypertension)
- Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole 1 SS tablet PO 3x/week (PCP prophylaxis, continued through at least month 12 post-transplant per protocol)
- Acyclovir 400 mg PO BID (HSV/VZV prophylaxis, continued post-transplant)
- Pilocarpine 5 mg PO TID (xerostomia from oral cGVHD)
- Cyclosporine 0.05% ophthalmic emulsion (Restasis) 1 drop OU BID (cGVHD dry eyes)
- Artificial tears PRN (dry eyes)
- Saliva substitutes as needed (xerostomia)
- Topical clobetasol 0.05% cream applied sparingly to affected skin areas (nearly discontinued as skin GVHD resolving)
- Dexamethasone 0.5 mg/5 mL oral rinse (oral GVHD; frequency now reduced to PRN given improvement)

ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies (NKDA)

SOCIAL HISTORY: Never-smoker. Alcohol: rare/none. Nigerian-American, born in Lagos, living in the United States for ~20 years. Married with two adult children. Licensed practical nurse (LPN), recently returned to part-time work approximately 2 months ago.

FAMILY HISTORY: Family history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes (maternal). No known family history of hematologic malignancy, bone marrow failure, or inherited cancer syndrome.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital Signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 78 bpm (regular), RR 16, Temp 36.8°C (98.2°F), SpO2 98% on room air. Weight: 71.2 kg (up ~3 kg from prior visit, appropriate weight recovery). BMI: 25.8 kg/m2. ECOG Performance Status: 1.

General: Ms. Okonkwo is a well-nourished, well-appearing woman in no acute distress. She is alert, oriented, and engages actively in discussion about her clinical status. Hair regrowth complete. Affect is pleasant and somewhat relieved.

Skin: Near-complete resolution of prior lichenoid changes on the forearms and dorsal hands. Faint residual hyperpigmented patches on the dorsal hands, without active erythema or scaling. No sclerodermatous changes. No new rashes. No petechiae or ecchymoses. Estimated < 5% BSA involved, inactive. Hair fully regrown (texture change noted by patient, considered normal post-chemotherapy).

Head/Face: Normocephalic, atraumatic. No facial puffiness or moon facies (appropriately off steroids).

Eyes: Mild conjunctival injection bilaterally, consistent with chronic dry eye syndrome. No corneal ulcerations on gross examination. On follow-up ophthalmology review (recent visit), no progression of ocular cGVHD. Schirmer's test stable. EOMI. No scleral icterus.

Oral Cavity: Oral mucosa moist but with mild residual xerostomia. No active lichenoid plaques or ulcerations. No oral candidiasis. Tongue smooth without lesions. Dentition intact. Salivary glands mildly firm bilaterally on palpation without tenderness.

Neck: Supple. No cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Thyroid non-enlarged, non-tender, without nodules.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. S1 and S2 normal. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.

Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rhonchi, or crackles. Good air movement. No dullness to percussion. Chest expansion symmetric.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Normal bowel sounds. Liver edge not palpable below the right costal margin. Spleen not palpable. No organomegaly. No ascites. No abdominal masses.

Extremities: No joint swelling, warmth, or tenderness. Full range of motion of all major joints. No contractures. No avascular necrosis signs on hip internal/external rotation. No lower extremity edema. Peripheral pulses 2+ bilaterally.

Neurological: Alert and oriented x4. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Strength 5/5 upper and lower extremities bilaterally. Gait normal, tandem walk normal (fully resolved from prior HiDAC cerebellar toxicity). Finger-nose-finger: no dysmetria. No nystagmus. Deep tendon reflexes 2+ and symmetric. Sensation intact to light touch and proprioception. No peripheral neuropathy.

Lymph Nodes: No palpable adenopathy in cervical, axillary, inguinal, or epitrochlear chains.

Musculoskeletal: No bony tenderness on palpation of spine, hips, or long bones.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS

Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 5,600/uL (differential: neutrophils 62%, lymphocytes 28%, monocytes 8%, eosinophils 1.5%, basophils 0.5%)
- ANC: 3,472/uL
- Hemoglobin: 12.8 g/dL (MCV 87 fL, MCH 29 pg)
- Hematocrit: 38.4%
- Platelets: 210,000/uL
- No blast forms or dysplastic morphology on differential

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium 138 mEq/L, Potassium 4.1 mEq/L, Chloride 103 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 25 mEq/L
- BUN 14 mg/dL, Creatinine 0.9 mg/dL (eGFR > 60 mL/min/1.73m2)
- AST 22 U/L, ALT 28 U/L, Alkaline Phosphatase 68 U/L, Total Bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL
- Albumin 4.0 g/dL, Total protein 7.1 g/dL
- Glucose 102 mg/dL (fasting)
- Calcium 9.3 mg/dL, Magnesium 1.9 mg/dL, Phosphorus 3.4 mg/dL

Additional Labs:
- Tacrolimus trough level: 4.1 ng/mL (on dose taper; appropriately at lower range given near-complete cGVHD resolution)
- HbA1c: 6.8% (type 2 DM well-controlled)
- LDH: 162 U/L (normal)
- Uric acid: 4.8 mg/dL (normal)
- CMV PCR (plasma): NOT DETECTED (negative; off valganciclovir since day +100)
- EBV PCR (plasma): NOT DETECTED
- Beta-D-glucan: negative (below detection threshold)
- Galactomannan: negative
- CMV IgG: positive (recipient prior exposure; expected)
- CD4 T-cell count: 380/uL (recovering appropriately; goal >200/uL for discontinuation of PCP prophylaxis; on track)
- CD8 T-cell count: 620/uL
- NK cells: 190/uL
- Total lymphocyte count by flow subset panel: within expected recovery range for 12 months post-PBSC transplant
- TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal)

Pulmonary Function Tests (performed today):
- FEV1: 88% predicted (1.98 L; prior: not formally tested post-transplant)
- FVC: 91% predicted (2.56 L)
- FEV1/FVC ratio: 0.77 (normal; no obstructive pattern, reassuringly no bronchiolitis obliterans)
- DLCO: 82% predicted (mildly reduced; likely represents residual effect of prior anthracycline/busulfan conditioning and/or subclinical pulmonary cGVHD, versus prior chemotherapy effect; no significant change from 6-month clinical assessment)
- TLC: 92% predicted (no restrictive pattern)
- Interpretation: Mild isolated reduction in DLCO without obstructive or restrictive spirometry pattern. No evidence of bronchiolitis obliterans. Clinical significance: monitoring; no intervention indicated at this time.

Bone Marrow Biopsy and Aspirate (performed today, results reviewed at this visit):
- Cellularity: 40% (normocellular for age; appropriate post-transplant recovery)
- Morphology: Normal trilineage hematopoiesis. Erythroid and myeloid maturation complete. Megakaryocytes present, adequate in number, with normal morphology. Myeloblasts by morphology: < 2% (no excess blasts). No Auer rods. No dysplastic features.
- Flow cytometry MRD (8-color panel): NEGATIVE — no leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected (sensitivity 0.01%)
- Cytogenetics (karyotype): 46,XX [20/20] — normal female karyotype, no del(7q), consistent with full donor engraftment
- Donor chimerism by STR analysis: Whole marrow 99% donor; T-cell fraction 99% donor; Myeloid fraction 99% donor
- FLT3-ITD by digital PCR: NOT DETECTED (below assay sensitivity of 0.01%)
- DNMT3A p.R882H by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED
- ASXL1 frameshift (G646Wfs*12) by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED
- SRSF2 p.P95H by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED
- Conclusion: Durable MRD-negative complete remission confirmed at 12 months post-alloHSCT with full donor chimerism and molecular clearance of all tracked somatic mutations.

---

IMAGING RESULTS

No new imaging performed at this visit (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis was performed at 6-month evaluation and showed no evidence of disease; per transplant surveillance protocol, repeat CT imaging is planned at 18 months if she remains clinically well). PET-CT was obtained pre-transplant and showed no evidence of metabolically active disease. No new imaging findings to report today.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Okonkwo is a 49-year-old woman with a history of adverse-risk AML-MRC (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, with DNMT3A/ASXL1/SRSF2/RUNX1 co-mutations, arising from antecedent MDS) who underwent 7+3 plus midostaurin induction with achievement of MRD-negative CR1, followed by one cycle of HiDAC consolidation, and then 10/10 MUD alloHSCT with reduced-intensity Flu/Bu conditioning. She is now at 12 months post-transplant and is doing exceptionally well by every objective and subjective measure.

1. AML-MRC, MRD-negative CR1 — 12 months post-alloHSCT
This is the most important item. Today's bone marrow biopsy confirms ongoing morphologic and molecular remission with MRD negativity by sensitive flow cytometry and ddPCR for all three tracked mutations (FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A R882H, ASXL1 G646Wfs*12, SRSF2 P95H). Full donor chimerism maintained at 99% across all lineages. CBC is entirely normal. This is the clinical picture we were hoping for — this patient with adverse-risk disease has now achieved one year of sustained MRD-negative remission post-transplant. The graft-versus-leukemia effect is likely contributing to this durable remission. She will continue protocol surveillance. No AML-directed therapy indicated or planned at this time.

Cancer present: NO. Disease status: Sustained MRD-negative CR1, 12 months post-alloHSCT.

2. Chronic GVHD — Mild, Resolving
By NIH global severity scoring, her chronic GVHD is now mild-to-resolving: skin involvement is < 5% BSA with only faint residual pigmentation changes (inactive by exam), oral involvement is in remission with only residual xerostomia managed symptomatically with pilocarpine and saliva substitutes, and ocular cGVHD is stable on topical cyclosporine drops. She has not required systemic steroids, which is reassuring and avoids the well-known metabolic and immunosuppressive complications of long-course steroid therapy. Given the excellent trajectory, the plan is to continue tacrolimus taper to 0.5 mg daily over the next 4-6 weeks, then discontinue entirely if she remains stable. Topical clobetasol can be tapered and discontinued given near-complete resolution of skin lesions. Oral dexamethasone rinse: continue PRN only. Pilocarpine: continue for xerostomia. Ophthalmology follow-up scheduled in 3 months.

3. Immunosuppression Taper and Prophylaxis Management
Tacrolimus 1 mg daily — taper to 0.5 mg daily over the next 4-6 weeks, then discontinue, barring any cGVHD flare.
PCP prophylaxis (TMP-SMX): CD4 count now 380/uL, above the 200/uL threshold for safe discontinuation. Given she is approaching complete discontinuation of tacrolimus and CD4 recovery is progressing, PCP prophylaxis may be safely discontinued after tacrolimus is fully stopped and she has been off immunosuppression for at least 4 weeks. Plan to discuss at 15-month visit.
Acyclovir: Continue through at least 18-24 months post-transplant per institutional protocol (VZV reactivation risk remains elevated).
No antifungal prophylaxis currently — appropriate given immune recovery and CD4 > 200/uL.
CMV and EBV PCR: both undetectable at 12 months, no active monitoring needed at monthly intervals; will check at 15-month visit.

4. Pulmonary Function — Mild DLCO Reduction
FEV1/FVC ratio is normal (0.77), ruling out bronchiolitis obliterans (the most feared pulmonary complication in the context of cGVHD). Mildly reduced DLCO at 82% predicted, likely reflecting a combination of residual chemotherapy effect (anthracycline, busulfan), prior conditioning-related injury, and/or mild subclinical pulmonary cGVHD. No clinical symptoms of dyspnea at rest. Will monitor DLCO at next formal PFT assessment (planned at 18-month evaluation). No intervention indicated. She should be encouraged to continue aerobic reconditioning, as this will help improve her functional capacity.

5. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus — Well-Controlled
HbA1c 6.8% — excellent control on metformin 1000 mg BID. Blood glucose 102 mg/dL fasting today. Continue current regimen. Fasting lipid panel to be ordered per cardiometabolic surveillance (late effects of transplant including metabolic syndrome are important long-term considerations). Dietary counseling reinforced.

6. Hypertension — Stable
BP 128/76 on lisinopril 10 mg daily — well-controlled. Continue current regimen. Renal function normal (Cr 0.9 mg/dL, eGFR preserved). Note: tacrolimus can exacerbate hypertension and renal dysfunction; as taper completes, blood pressure and renal function should improve or stabilize. Will monitor BP and Cr at 15-month visit.

7. Late Effects Surveillance
No avascular necrosis symptoms (no hip, knee, or shoulder pain) — clinical screen negative. Will recommend DXA bone density scan (deferred today; schedule at 15-18 month window, given off steroids). Ophthalmology: cataract surveillance at annual exam. Secondary malignancy surveillance: no clinical concerns today; skin exam performed (no new lesions). Thyroid function normal (TSH 2.1). Reproductive health: Patient noted to have ongoing amenorrhea post-transplant conditioning — referral to reproductive endocrinology / endocrinology to assess for premature ovarian insufficiency (POI) should be placed if she has not already been evaluated; this is standard of care in a 49-year-old post-conditioning.

8. Vaccinations
Post-transplant vaccination schedule: inactivated vaccines were resumed per protocol. Today's vaccines administered: Influenza (inactivated), diphtheria/tetanus (Td), and pneumococcal (PCV20, if not yet completed in the post-transplant schedule). Live vaccines remain contraindicated until at least 24 months post-transplant with full immune recovery and off all immunosuppression. MMR and varicella deferred at this time.

9. Quality of Life and Psychosocial
Patient scores substantially improved on FACT-BMT at this visit compared to her 6-month assessment. She has returned to part-time nursing work, which is a remarkable milestone and reflects her functional recovery. She acknowledges some ongoing anxiety around disease relapse (appropriate, given the intensity of her illness and treatment), and this was discussed openly. She is encouraged to continue with any counseling or support group resources she has found helpful. Progressive return to full-time work is encouraged as tolerated.

10. Follow-Up
- Next visit: approximately 3 months (15-month post-transplant evaluation)
- At that visit: CBC, CMP, tacrolimus trough (if not yet discontinued), CMV/EBV PCR, CD4 count; reassess cGVHD status; DXA ordering discussion; discuss discontinuation of TMP-SMX prophylaxis
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: planned at 18-month post-transplant surveillance time point
- Bone marrow biopsy: per protocol, planned at 18 months (repeat chimerism and MRD)
- Ophthalmology follow-up: 3 months
- Reproductive endocrinology referral: to be placed today for POI evaluation
- Pulmonary function tests: repeat at 18-month evaluation

Signed: Hematology/BMT Attending, with BMT Fellow
